blocks.body: Pronouns and demonstratives are an important part of English grammar. Here you will learn how to correctly use personal pronouns (I, you, he, she, it, we, they), possessive forms (my, mine, your, yours), reflexive pronouns (myself, yourself), as well as indicative words (this, that, these, those) and other types of pronouns.

• Subject pronouns — subject pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they.
  • Object pronouns - object pronouns: me, you, him, her, it, us, them.
  • Subject pronouns perform the action: She reads books.
  • Object pronouns receive action: He called me.
blocks.heading: Personal Pronouns (Personal Pronouns)

blocks.body:
• Possessive adjectives — possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.
  • Possessive pronouns — possessive pronouns: mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs.
  • Possessive adjectives come before a noun: This is my book.
  • Possessive pronouns replace a noun: This book is mine.
blocks.heading: Possessive Forms

blocks.body:
• Forms: myself, yourself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, themselves.
  • Used when the subject and object are the same person: She taught herself Spanish.
  • Emphatic use — for emphasis: I did it myself.
  • By + reflexive = independently: He lives by himself.
blocks.heading: Reflexive Pronouns)

blocks.body:
Topic Level Description
Personal Pronouns A1 Personal and object pronouns
Possessive Forms A1–A2 Possessive adjectives and pronouns
Reflexive Pronouns A2–B1 Reflexive pronouns (myself, myself)
Demonstrative Pronouns A1 Indicative words (this, that, these, those)
Indefinite Pronouns A2–B1 Indefinite pronouns (someone, anybody, nothing)
Relative Pronouns B1 Relative pronouns (who, which, that, whose)
blocks.heading: Topics in this section

blocks.body:
• This / These — for close objects: This is my pen. These are my books.
  • That / Those - for distant objects: That is your car. Those are your keys.
  • This/That is singular, these/those is plural.
  • Can be both adjectives and pronouns: I like this book. I like this.
blocks.heading: Demonstratives

blocks.body:
🧠

Start with personal pronouns - they are used in every sentence.

Remember the difference between my (possessive adjective) and mine (possessive pronoun).

Reflexive pronouns always end in -self (singular) or -selves (plural).

This/that for singular, these/those for plural - an easy way to avoid mistakes.
blocks.heading: Learning tips
